does: This paper describes the design and implementation of safety mechanisms built into Sora 2, a state-of-the-art video generation model, and its associated social creation platform. The work documents concrete protective measures integrated from the ground up into both the model and the application layer, addressing novel safety challenges unique to advanced video synthesis capabilities. The authors present their approach to safety as foundational rather than afterthought, detailing specific protections against harmful content generation and misuse on a platform designed for creative sharing.
connects: This work extends prior research on safety in generative AI systems, building on established practices from image generation models and large language models while addressing the distinct challenges posed by video synthesis. It responds to growing concerns about deepfakes, unauthorized content creation, and platform harms documented in recent literature on synthetic media. The paper builds on safety frameworks that have evolved across OpenAI's product line, incorporating lessons from text and image moderation while innovating to handle temporal coherence and motion-based harms that are unique to video generation.
enables: This work establishes foundations for safer deployment of advanced video generation capabilities to mass-market users, potentially enabling broader creative applications while maintaining safety guardrails. It points toward future research on real-time content moderation systems that can evaluate synthetic video, detection methods for AI-generated content, and platform design patterns for generative tools. The safety infrastructure described here could inform how subsequent generations of multimodal generative systems balance capability with risk mitigation, and may enable safer integration of video synthesis into existing social platforms and creative workflows.
